title: Unit 05 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Ethical Behavior & Digital Responsibility.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of digital ethics?

choices.text: To increase the number of posts shared online
choices.feedback: Digital ethics focuses on responsible behavior, not increasing activity.

choices.text: To guide responsible and respectful behavior when using technology
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital ethics provides guidance for making responsible online decisions.

choices.text: To prevent all online communication
choices.feedback: Digital ethics supports responsible use, not avoidance.

choices.text: To allow unrestricted sharing of information
choices.feedback: Ethics involves limits and responsibility, not unrestricted behavior.

questions.prompt: Which example best represents fair use of content?

choices.text: Copying an entire article and posting it as your own
choices.feedback: This is plagiarism, not fair use.

choices.text: Using a small portion of content with proper attribution for educational purposes
choices.feedback: Correct. Fair use often allows limited use with attribution for learning.

choices.text: Downloading and sharing copyrighted material without permission
choices.feedback: Sharing without permission violates copyright.

choices.text: Using any content found online without credit
choices.feedback: Attribution is required in most cases.

questions.prompt: Plagiarism includes using someone else's work without giving credit.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Plagiarism occurs when work is used without proper attribution.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Using work without credit is a clear example of plagiarism.

questions.prompt: Which action could lead to legal consequences online?

choices.text: Respecting copyright rules
choices.feedback: Following rules reduces risk, not increases it.

choices.text: Giving credit to original creators
choices.feedback: Attribution is a responsible action.

choices.text: Sharing copyrighted material without permission
choices.feedback: Correct. Unauthorized sharing can lead to legal consequences.

choices.text: Creating original content
choices.feedback: Creating original content does not violate laws.

questions.prompt: What is an important ethical consideration when using AI tools?

choices.text: Using AI to replace all personal effort
choices.feedback: AI should support learning, not replace responsibility.

choices.text: Using AI without checking accuracy
choices.feedback: AI outputs should always be verified.

choices.text: Using AI responsibly and ensuring work remains accurate and original
choices.feedback: Correct. Ethical AI use requires accuracy, honesty, and responsibility.

choices.text: Submitting AI-generated work as entirely your own without review
choices.feedback: This raises issues of academic integrity.

questions.prompt: A student copies part of a website into an assignment but includes a citation. What is the best evaluation?

choices.text: It is acceptable because the source was credited
choices.feedback: Correct. Proper citation can make use of content acceptable, depending on context.

choices.text: It is always plagiarism even with citation
choices.feedback: Citation distinguishes proper use from plagiarism.

choices.text: It is only acceptable if no one notices
choices.feedback: Ethical decisions are not based on whether someone notices.

choices.text: It is acceptable only if the entire article is copied
choices.feedback: Copying entire works is typically not acceptable.

questions.prompt: Digital actions can have legal and academic consequences.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Online behavior can result in real-world consequences.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Digital actions are subject to rules and consequences.

questions.prompt: Which question best supports ethical decision-making online?

choices.text: Will this get more attention?
choices.feedback: Attention is not the key factor in ethical decisions.

choices.text: Is this allowed and respectful to others?
choices.feedback: Correct. Ethical decisions consider rules and respect for others.

choices.text: Will others copy me?
choices.feedback: Other people’s actions are not the primary factor.

choices.text: Is this the fastest option?
choices.feedback: Speed does not determine ethical behavior.
